proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +32.0pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (D-8 review pending); promotion remains capped at TAKE_SMALL until that resolves
Portfolio shows 100% Choppy regime concentration — if regime flips to Bear, this cohort will activate into an already-stressed book and trader should reassess sizing at that transition
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +32.0pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +32.0pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with zero Bear exposure in 43 open positions, so activation is deferred and the existing 100% Choppy concentration is not compounded. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-13) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +25.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio regime concentration at 100% Choppy with 3 concentrations flagged — any UP_TRI/Metal match would add to an already over-concentrated book
R-multiple 0.72 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (D-8 under review); promotion remains TAKE_SMALL-only pending resolution
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +25.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +25.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies UP_TRI exposure (13 of 43 open) in a 100% Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-13) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime = Choppy (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime = Choppy; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) cohort_health for Choppy×DOWN_TRI or Choppy×UP_TRI promotes to Tier M, or (b) regime concentration drops below 0.80 via natural Day 6 exits. Prioritize allowing DOWN_TRI×FMCG and DOWN_TRI×Energy positions to age out before adding new shorts.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bull regime flip would simultaneously invalidate the entire 43-position book — particularly the 29 DOWN_TRI shorts concentrated in FMCG/Energy/Auto — with no statistical edge backstop since the dominant cohort is only Candidate tier. Sector-clustered shorts (FMCG 6, Energy 5, Auto 6) would unwind together on a broad risk-on shift.

evidence: regime_concentration triggered: Choppy observed 1.0 vs threshold 0.80 (43/43 open positions)
Matched Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort is Candidate tier (n=44) — unvalidated edge
Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort is Candidate tier (n=107) — unvalidated edge
DOWN_TRI dominates signal mix at 29/43 = 67.4%, reinforcing directional short skew within unvalidated regime
Top cells (FMCG 13.95%, IT 11.63%, Energy 11.63%) all below 20% threshold but cluster within Choppy regime
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) cohort_health for Choppy×DOWN_TRI or Choppy×UP_TRI promotes to Tier M, or (b) regime concentration drops below 0.80 via natural Day 6 exits. Prioritize allowing DOWN_TRI×FMCG and DOWN_TRI×Energy positions to age out before adding new shorts.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bull regime flip would simultaneously invalidate the entire 43-position book — particularly the 29 DOWN_TRI shorts concentrated in FMCG/Energy/Auto — with no statistical edge backstop since the dominant cohort is only Candidate tier. Sector-clustered shorts (FMCG 6, Energy 5, Auto 6) would unwind together on a broad risk-on shift.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (observed 1.0, above the 0.80 threshold) and the matched Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=44) — unvalidated edge. DOWN_TRI dominates signal mix at 29/43 = 67.4% (below 85%) and no single cell breaches 20% (top is FMCG at 13.95%), but the regime concentration alone with unvalidated cohort support warrants warn — a Choppy→Bull flip would unwind the short-heavy book simultaneously.
